message:
fix(export): add cache-busting to HTML script tags for GitHub Pages

The previous fix added cache-busting to data fetches in viewer.js,
but the CDN was still serving the OLD cached viewer.js file itself,
so the fix never took effect on updates.

This fix adds cache-busting query parameters (?v=TIMESTAMP) to the
script src attributes in index.html at export time:
- viewer.js, charts.js, graph.js, hybrid_scorer.js, wasm_loader.js

Now when a user deploys an update:
1. New index.html has ?v=NEW_TIMESTAMP on script tags
2. Browser requests viewer.js?v=NEW_TIMESTAMP (cache miss)
3. New viewer.js loads with its cache-busting for data files
4. Fresh database is fetched and displayed correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
